question:
Will Gen.G win LoL Worlds 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the esports team winning 1st place in the League of Legends 2025 Season World Championship (Worlds 2025).

If the winner of the World Cup 2025 is not determined by December 31, 2025 ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Liquipedia: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

evidences.title: GEN vs. G2 — MSI 2025 — Bracket Stage
evidences.content: The 2025 League of Legends Mid-Season Invitational is here—bringing together the five regions to battle for glory, pride, and a critical edge heading into Worlds. As the second international showdown on the LoL Esports calendar, MSI pits two representatives from each regional league against one another in a high-stakes clash that tests their mettle at the season’s halfway mark. More than just a trophy is on the line: the winning team and the next best-performing region will earn extra slots at the World Championship, raising the stakes even higher. This year, the action unfolds beginning June 27 at the iconic Pacific Coliseum in Vancouver, Canada. Get ready for rivalries, redemption arcs, and unforgettable plays as pros will be challenged to Ignite The Fire Within. [...] # GEN vs. G2 — MSI 2025 — Bracket Stage ## LoL Esports 3930000 subscribers 6965 likes ### Description 973853 views Posted: 2 Jul 2025 MSI 2025, LIVE FROM VANCOUVER #msi2025

evidences.title: Gen.G announce full roster for LCK 2025
evidences.content: ### Gen.G's full roster for the LCK 2025 season: Top: Kim "Kiin" Gi-in Jgl: Kim "Canyon" Geon-bu Mid: Jeong "Chovy"Ji-hoon ADC: Park "Ruler"Jae-hyuk Supp: Joo "Duro"Min-kyu With the level of domination team put up in the LCK in 2024, the bar will be high for this new Gen.G roster coming into 2025. A single international trophy, however, can prove to be the difference in the end. Follow Strafe Esports for all the latest League news and our X account for the latest content and coverages. Also, stay tuned to Strafe YouTube for exclusive interviews, press conferences, and more. For more League of Legends rostermania news, check out ourLEC, LCK , and LTA North/South rostermanias for all the latest shuffles. During the 2024 LCK Summer Season, Kiin led all top laners in KDA, while also playing the most unique champions out of all players in his position. evidences.title: Gen.G - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Before the start of the 2024 season the team went through several changes, with Choi "Doran" Hyeon-joon, Han "Peanut" Wang-ho, and Yoo "Delight" Hwan-joong leaving the roster to join Hanwha Life Esports. To replace them, the organisation signed Kim "Kiin" Gi-in, Kim "Canyon" Geon-bu, and Son "Lehends" Si-woo. The roster found immediate success after the organisation won its fourth consecutive LCK title in the 2024 LCK Spring Split final, defeating T1 3–2 in a close series which allowed them to qualify for the 2024 Mid-Season Invitational. There, the roster made it to the grand final of the event after defeating Bilibili Gaming 3–1 in the upper bracket final. In the Summer split, all 5 members of Gen.G were named to the All-LCK First Team, demonstrating their high level performance [...] 27. ↑ Leonardo Biazzi (November 29, 2023). "Gen.G builds incredible new LoL roster around star mid laner Chovy". Dot Esports. Retrieved May 17, 2024. 28. ↑ David Jang (November 30, 2023). "Gen.G Unveils its 2024 Roster and Coaching Staff". Inven Global. Retrieved May 17, 2024. 29. ↑ David Jang (April 14, 2024). "Gen.G secures historic 4-Peat, Kiin named Finals MVP". Inven Global. Retrieved May 16...

evidences.title: WORLDS 2024 LCK TEAMS BREAKDOWN - MAJOR REGION RUNDOWN FT CHRONICLER
evidences.content: Champion Zeka in the mid lane, another World Champion at AD Carry, Viper, and Delight as their support. Hanwha Life Esports last participated in Worlds 2021 with a very different roster back then, with Chovy and Deft. After missing out on Worlds 2023, Hanwha Life decided to build around their World Championship carries, Zeka and Viper, adding in Doran, Peanut, and Delight from the domestically dominant Gen.G, forming a super team. The team has looked solid throughout the year. In Spring, they finished the Regular Season in 3rd, with a 15-3 record, and they beat T1 in the 2nd round of playoffs. But they lost to Gen.G and T1 in the rematch. In Summer, they took advantage of the slumping T1 to solidify themselves as the 2nd best team in Korea, and they proved it in the playoffs by beating [...] in Korea, and they proved it in the playoffs by beating the 2023 World Champions twice. Going into the Finals, everyone expected them to lose, as Gen.G has had their number consistently. Not only have they defeated them in the six encounters of 2024, Hanwha Life Esports as an organization was also on an 19 consecutive losing streak against Gen.G. Alongside the fact that Chovy actually had a...

evidences.title: Chovy and Ruler sign three-year contract with Gen.G
evidences.content: Sofia Guimarães Esports Writer Sofia is a Porto-based esports journalist who turned a childhood of watching her brother play into a career covering the industry. When she’s not playing League of Legends, TFT, or VALORANT, she’s diving into… Read full bio → Published Read time 2 min League of Legends News Chovy and Ruler sign three-year contract with Gen.G Gen.G closed their lineup for 2025 with an incredible roaster, renewing with Kiin, Canyon, and Chovy, and adding Ruler and Duro to the team. [...] ## Kiin, Canyon, and Chovy will keep going As for the rest of the team, Kim “Kiin” Gi-in, Kim “Canyon” Geon-bu, and Chovy renewed their contracts with Gen.G, being part of the lineup for 2025. Like Ruler, Chovy will be playing for the Korean team for three more years. They were dominant in the first LCK split, reached the finals in the second split, and fought in League of Legends Worlds 2024 (LoL Worlds). evidences.title: Worlds 2025 Format Explained: Everything You Need To Know
evidences.content: Sep 02 - Sep 04 $0 | Europe Intel Extreme Masters Beijing 2026: Global Qualifier Sep 02 - Sep 04 $0 | Europe Thunderpick World Championship 2026: Closed Qualifier Sep 09 - Sep 13 $0 | Europe OCT ESL Pro League Season 24 Oct 03 - Oct 11 $300,000 | Katowice Thunderpick World Championship 2026 Oct 12 - Oct 19 Malta , making the series a lot more exciting for both online and offline viewers. Fearless Draft Lol Worlds 2025 Image Credit: Riot Games The other change is the reduction of the Worlds teams from 20 to 17. This was caused by the merger of the minor regions in the Pacific to form one big region, the LCP, as well as the merger of the North American and South American leagues, forming the LTA. [...] Each of these two regions will field a total of three teams in this tournament, just like the LEC. The LPL and the LCK, after being the top two regions at MSI, were awarded a fourth additional slot, although the two #4 seeds will have to battle it out in the play-in stage to qualify for the Swiss stage, the first official stage of Worlds 2025. According to the official schedule, the Swiss stage will kick off on October 15. ## Worlds 2025 Format is split into 3 stages ### Stage 1: Pl...

evidences.title: League of Legends World Championship - Wikipedia
evidences.content: The finals were played at the Gocheok Sky Dome on 19 November 2023 between Weibo Gaming from the League of Legends Pro League (LPL), and previous years' finalist T1 "T1 (esports)") from the League of Legends Champions Korea (LCK). In the final, T1 defeated Weibo Gaming 3–0, extending their record for the most world championships won by single team with four titles. T1's top laner, Choi "Zeus" Woo-je "Zeus (gamer)"), was named the MVP of the series, while teammate Faker became the first player to win four World Championships. ### 2024 [edit] Main article: 2024 League of Legends World Championship [...] | Tournament winners | 2011: Fnatic 2012: Taipei Assassins 2013: SKT T1 "T1 (esports)") 2014: Samsung Galaxy White "Samsung Galaxy (esports)") 2015: SKT T1 "T1 (esports)") 2016: SKT T1 "T1 (esports)") 2017: Samsung Galaxy "Samsung Galaxy (esports)") 2018: Invictus Gaming 2019: FunPlus Phoenix 2020: Damwon Gaming 2021: Edward Gaming 2022: DRX "DRX (esports)") 2023: T1 "T1 (esports)") 2024: T1 "T1 (esports)") 2025: T1 "T1 (esports)") | [...] Main article: 2024 League of Legends World Championship The 2024 World Championship was held from 25 September to 2 November 2024, with the even...

evidences.title: 2025 LoL World Championship Details Revealed - Inven Global
evidences.content: The event will open with a best-of-five Play-In series between Korea’s T1 and China’s Invictus Gaming. The winner will move on to the Swiss Stage, while the loser will be eliminated. Riot emphasized the tournament’s theme, “Earn Your Legacy,” underscoring the idea that championship glory must be won rather than inherited. ## League of Legends Marks 15th Anniversary with 2025 World Championship in China The Swiss Stage, running October 15–25, will host 16 teams across multiple regions, including Gen.G, Hanwha Life Esports, kt Rolster, and T1 from Korea. Korea’s LCK enters the tournament seeking a fourth consecutive Worlds title, having claimed victories each year since 2022. With nine championships to date, another win would make the LCK the first region to reach double-digit titles. [...] China’s representatives include Bilibili Gaming, Anyone’s Legend, Top Esports, and Invictus Gaming. Europe, the Middle East and Africa will send G2 Esports, Movistar KOI, and Fnatic, while North and South America will be represented by FlyQuest, VIVO Keyd Stars, and 100 Thieves. Asia-Pacific slots will go to CTBC Flying Oyster, Team Secret Whales, and PSG Talon. The Knockout Stage will be held...

evidences.title: Power ranking the League of Legends World Championship contenders - ESPN
evidences.content: 4. G2 Esports No one player in the League European Championship's been better this summer than G2 Esports mid laner and league MVP Rasmus "Caps" Winther. After a shaky regular season in both spring and summer, G2 Esports bounced back in the lower bracket of the LEC summer playoffs and then avenged their loss to Fnatic in the grand final. Along the way, Caps had banner performances and Martin "Wunder" Hansen also performed incredibly well. Compared to the world finalists G2 were in 2019, this team does look weaker -- especially as Luka "Perkz" Perković and Marcin "Jankos" Jankowski haven't had as good of a 2020 as the year before -- but their group heading to worlds looks ripe for the taking as the best team Europe has to offer. -- Jacob Wolf 5. Gen.G [...] LGD Gaming (if they make it out of play-ins), will be a tough one for this Gen.G team. -- Wolf [...] After playing in the first seven world championships, TSM finally return to the dance for the first time in three years. Yiliang "Doublelift" Peng left Team Liquid before the start of summer split to reunite with his bot lane partner Vincent "Biofrost" Wang and mid laner Søren "Bjergsen" Bjerg. The team overcame the odds in sum...

evidences.title: All LoL Worlds winners throughout the years - esports.gg
evidences.content: The Korean team DAMWON Gaming (previously DWG KIA, now named Dplus Kia) defeated the Chinese team Suning by 3-1. The MVP was the jungler Kim “Canyon” Geon-bu, nowadays playing for Gen.G. DAMWON Gaming won the Worlds 2020 (Image via Riot Games) ## Worlds 2021 The winners of 2020 couldn’t keep the victory for another year in the Worlds. The Chinese team EDward Gaming won against DWG KIA by 3-2. The finals were in Laugardalshöll Sports Center, Reykjavík, Iceland. The MVP of this year was the Korean mid-laner Lee “Scout” Ye-chan, now a player of LNG, who was in the middle of a controversy due to his inability to leave China for the Worlds 2024. EDward Gaming won the Worlds 2021 (Images via Riot Games) ## Worlds 2022 [...] ## Worlds 2022 The Chase Center in San Francisco was a special place for the Koreans DRX in 2022. More than 70% of the fans, according to Riot, thought that T1 was going to win the Worlds 2022. However, when DRX was losing 2-0 and everything seemed lost, they did a reverse sweep and won the Worlds by 3-2. The top laner Hwang “Kingen” Seong-hoon was elected the MVP of the event. DRX won the Worlds 2022 (Image via Riot Games) ## Worlds 2023 In the baseball stadium, G...

Fun plus Phoenix stunned the world with their unconventional gameplay, leaving opponents struggling to find answers and ultimately helping China secure backto back summoner cups. evidences.title: 2025 LCK season - Wikipedia
evidences.content: In line with Riot Games' announcement of a new split structure and competitive calendar for League of Legends esports beginning with the 2025 competitive season, the LCK announced on 29 October 2024 that the former Spring and Summer Splits would be merged into one season, with the 2025 Mid-Season Invitational in between them, and the creation of the LCK Cup to serve as a qualifier for the inaugural First Stand Tournament. Additionally, the last three rounds of the season taking place after MSI 2025 will feature a triple round-robin group stage and a season champion will be crowned, with results from Rounds 1 and 2 having implications for teams in Rounds 3 to 5.

evidences.title: Gen.G Esports - Liquipedia League of Legends Wiki
evidences.content: Gen.G are a League of Legends team featuring players such as Kiin, Canyon, Chovy, Ruler, and Duro. The page also includes historical team images and past achievements such as LCK Spring 2024 and MSI 2024 titles.
